etymology.description: 直(直) (zhí, "looking forward") + 心(心) (xīn, "heart, mind") = looking forward with the heart -> virtue
etymology.interpretation: The character 惪 combines the concept of being straightforward or upright (直) with the heart or mind (心), suggesting that virtue involves aligning one’s intentions and actions in a sincere and sincere manner. This concept of integrity is central to the traditional Chinese understanding of virtue.
